message: gamma export left briefed slide(s) unmatched for variant A: ['slide-02']; unmatched pages: ['70percent']

source_points.verbatim_text: This short lesson is deliberately narrow.
Rather than survey many trends, we anchor everything to one decisive workforce number and what it means for clinical leadership.
70% of frontline clinicians report at least one symptom of professional burnout.

dissents.marcus_position: typed src-001-c002 (Slide 1) as other; a defensible alternative reading is narration

pedagogy_annotations.rationale: Displays the headline 70% burnout prevalence to be memorized; as a definition slide it introduces the single figure students must recall and know how to read (lo-g0-001, lo-g0-006).
Narration motivates anchoring on one decisive workforce number, ties 70% to leadership relevance, and clarifies interpretation, building understanding (lo-g0-002, lo-g0-005, lo-g0-006).
Shows example decisions and messaging that start with the 70% figure, modeling application to staffing/workflow and leading in communication (lo-g0-003, lo-g0-004).
Narration integrates the 70% anchor into staffing/workflow decisions and models leading with it in comms while keeping focus on the single figure (lo-g0-003, lo-g0-004, lo-g0-005).

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: A two-unit micro-lesson that first narrows learner attention to one decisive workforce number, then presents and reinforces the single headline statistic: 70% of frontline clinicians report at least one symptom of professional burnout. Scope is intentionally constrained to framing and recall of that one figure, with no expansion into additional trends, causes, interventions, or leadership actions beyond what the source explicitly states.

cd_directive.creative_rationale: This lesson is built to make one number do all the work: a clean title card followed by a single, oversized “70%” with minimal captioning and no competing statistics. A visual-led profile foregrounds that numeral, leaning on high creative weight to deliver immediacy and salience while keeping text sparse and adaptive. Source-grounded, professional narration can reiterate the 70% and connect it to leadership decisions without diluting the slide’s focus, and balanced coupling keeps eyes on the figure itself. This preserves the one-number frame, maximizes recall, and matches the intended high-impact, uncluttered viewing experience.

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: A two-unit lesson built entirely around a one-number frame. Unit 1 narrows learner attention and establishes that the lesson will not survey multiple trends. Unit 2 delivers the sole headline statistic—70% of frontline clinicians report at least one symptom of professional burnout—and positions it as the number learners should remember and use when explaining the lesson to colleagues. No additional topic expansion beyond this framing and statistic is planned because the source corpus does not support further content.

output.additional_instructions: Experience profile: visual-led. Creative rationale: This lesson is built to make one number do all the work: a clean title card followed by a single, oversized “70%” with minimal captioning and no competing statistics. A visual-led profile foregrounds that numeral, leaning on high creative weight to deliver immediacy and salience while keeping text sparse and adaptive. Source-grounded, professional narration can reiterate the 70% and connect it to leadership decisions without diluting the slide’s focus, and balanced coupling keeps eyes on the figure itself. This preserves the one-number frame, maximizes recall, and matches the intended high-impact, uncluttered viewing experience. Slide mode proportions: creative=0.6, literal-text=0.15, literal-visual=0.25.
output.prompt:
Why One Number Frames This Lesson — Orient the learner to this lesson's narrow scope: one decisive workforce number will anchor the full leadership argument.

The 70% Clinician Burnout Figure — Recall and explain that 70% of frontline clinicians report at least one symptom of professional burnout, and recognize it as the lesson's headline figure.
